title: Academic Citations
description: When to quote, paraphrase, cite; avoiding plagiarism; integrating sources smoothly.
summary_pt_br: Citações: aspas pouco, paráfrase muito, sempre cite. Verbos: argues, claims, suggests, demonstrates.

scenes.subtitle_en: When to quote, paraphrase, cite; avoiding plagiarism; integrating sources smoothly.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Citações: aspas pouco, paráfrase muito, sempre cite. Verbos: argues, claims, suggests, demonstrates.

scenes.text_en: When to quote, paraphrase, cite; avoiding plagiarism; integrating sources smoothly. Today's focus: academic citations. Smith (2022) argues that remote work reshapes productivity.
scenes.text_pt: Citações: aspas pouco, paráfrase muito, sempre cite. Verbos: argues, claims, suggests, demonstrates. Foco de hoje: academic citations. Smith (2022) argumenta que o home office remodela a produtividade.

scenes.title: Academic Citations
scenes.body_en: Use direct quotes sparingly. Paraphrase most of the time. Always cite the source. Integrating verbs: argues, claims, suggests, demonstrates, contends.
scenes.body_pt: Citações: aspas pouco, paráfrase muito, sempre cite. Verbos: argues, claims, suggests, demonstrates.

examples.en: Smith (2022) argues that remote work reshapes productivity.
examples.pt: Smith (2022) argumenta que o home office remodela a produtividade.

examples.en: According to recent research, attention spans are shrinking.
examples.pt: De acordo com pesquisa recente, a atenção está encurtando.

examples.en: As one scholar puts it, 'information is not knowledge.'
examples.pt: Como diz um estudioso, 'informação não é conhecimento'.

exercise.question: Complete: "Smith (2022) argues that remote work reshapes ___."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Smith (2022) argues that remote work reshapes productivity."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Smith (2022) argumenta que o home office remodela a produtividade.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: According to recent research, attention spans are shrinking.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "According to recent research, attention spans are shrinking."
exercise.hint_pt_br: De acordo com pesquisa recente, a atenção está encurtando.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Academic Citations — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre academic citations. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: When should you use a direct quote?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Only when the wording itself is unique.
turns.pt_hint: Só se a frase é única.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: For most of your sources, in your own words.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do you introduce a source smoothly?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What's the danger of forgetting to cite?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It becomes plagiarism, even if unintended.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do you balance your own voice and sources?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Cite the idea, analyze it in your own words.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Citations turn borrowed light into your own fire.

scenes.title: The research paper review
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize Kumar's advice. Use at least two reporting verbs and one paraphrased sentence.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Kumar sat with a PhD student whose first paper was strong — but dangerously thin on citations.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Smith argues that attention has shrunk by forty percent in a decade — but where is the original study?

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: I paraphrased from three articles, but I only cited one because the others said similar things.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Parallel findings still require separate citations, even when the claims overlap substantially.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So every major claim needs a source, and every direct quote deserves an in-text reference?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Academic writing, Kumar often said, is a long conversation — and every speaker deserves to be introduced.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Academic Citations". When to quote, paraphrase, cite; avoiding plagiarism; integrating sources smoothly. Smith (2022) argues that remote work reshapes productivity. According to recent research, attention spans are shrinking. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Academic Citations". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: BBC News · Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Análise de notícias reais da BBC.

sources.label: TED Talks with subtitles
sources.pt_hint: Palestras reais com legenda — ouça, leia, repita.

sources.label: Cambridge Dictionary · thesaurus
sources.pt_hint: Sinônimos com exemplos — essencial em B2+.
